race.name: Race Around Netherlands

vitals.date_specific: 2026 edition: started May 2, 08:00, from De Proloog, Amerongen; cutoff May 10, 18:00 (202 hours). Next classic-loop edition scheduled for 6 May 2028; a separate gravel variant, RATN GX, is scheduled for 12 June 2027.
vitals.field_size: 50+ entrants (2026 results list well over 50; the '20-50' previously listed understated the field)
vitals.start_format: Solo or teams of up to 4, fully self-supported/unsupported — no outside material assistance or drafting, though ordinary public services (shops, hotels) are permitted
vitals.cutoff_time: 202 hours (8 days 10 hours): May 2 08:00 start to May 10 18:00 cutoff

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 64. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 60. NOT re-scored during 2026-07-24 editorial wave 9 — dimensions/overall_score left as-is, though the climbing=1 dimension was set against a null elevation figure; the corrected ~7,000m total (see vitals) is not trivial for a flat-country loop and a human may want to revisit this dimension in a future scoring pass.

course_description.character: A fixed, anticlockwise loop of roughly 1,950 km and 7,000 m of climbing, starting and finishing at De Proloog in Amerongen — 99% paved. This is a fully self-supported/unsupported ultra-distance race: outside material assistance and drafting are prohibited, though ordinary public services available to everyone (shops, hotels) are permitted. A shorter North Loop variant of roughly 1,315 km / 800 m is also offered. Organized by Germany-based Adventure Bike Racing; De Proloog is the host venue in Amerongen, not the organizing body. No connection found to RAAM, LEL, or Dutch professional road races.
course_description.signature_challenge: Completing the full ~1,950 km Dutch loop unsupported inside the 202-hour (8 day, 10 hour) cutoff — a test of self-sufficiency and pacing across a flat-country distance event, not a single climb or sector.
suffering_zones: The self-support constraint: no outside crew or material assistance allowed for over 1,950 km, meaning every mechanical or nutrition problem is the rider's own to solve using only public resources.
The closing days into the May 10 cutoff, when accumulated sleep debt over a week-plus effort meets a hard 202-hour clock with no neutralization.

logistics.lodging_strategy: Fully self-supported; optional hostel add-on night before race (May 1) at the De Proloog venue in Amerongen; no finish-line sleep provided.
logistics.food: None provided on course — fully self-supported/unsupported; riders rely on ordinary public services (shops, hotels) along the route.

history.origin_story: Organized by Germany-based Adventure Bike Racing (Michael Wacker); results published continuously since 2018. Runs on a roughly biennial cadence for the classic full loop, with a separate gravel variant (RATN GX) added to the calendar in off years.
notable_moments: 2026: Tiemen Van Tuinen wins as first full-loop solo finisher, 3 days 18 hours 10 minutes.

biased_opinion.verdict: A Self-Supported Ultra, Mislabeled as a Gran Fondo
biased_opinion.summary: Race Around The Netherlands is an active self-supported ultra-distance race — roughly 1,950 km and 7,000 m of climbing on a fixed anticlockwise loop from Amerongen, with results on record since at least 2018, organized by Germany-based Adventure Bike Racing. The 2026 edition ran May 2-10 as scheduled, with a first full-loop solo finisher (Tiemen Van Tuinen) recorded in provisional/unofficial results. This listing's prior data undersold and misdescribed it: no elevation figure was on file at all, the field size was capped at '20-50' when 2026 results list well over 50 entrants, and the attached video/rider-intel content was entirely unrelated to this event (Dutch national championship road-race footage, a Cape Town wind-cancellation clip, generic Dutch touring videos) — none of it describes this unsupported ultra-distance loop. The more fundamental issue is taxonomy: this is a self-supported, multi-day ultra-cycling race, not a one-day gran fondo, and doesn't fit cleanly into a database scored on single-day-fondo dimensions like field_depth or organization support.
strengths: A continuous, multi-year track record (results on file since at least 2018) with a completed, verifiable 2026 edition
A real distance/climbing total for a famously flat country — ~1,950 km / ~7,000 m on the full loop, plus a shorter ~1,315 km North Loop option, though the climbing-per-km is modest
Clear, documented rules (self-supported, no drafting, ordinary public services permitted) rather than vague marketing language
weaknesses: Self-supported ultra format is a poor fit for a database built around scored, one-day gran fondos — dimensions like field_depth, organization, and value don't map cleanly onto an unsupported multi-day race
Currently on a roughly biennial cadence for the classic full loop (next in 2028) — long gaps between opportunities to enter
No outside material assistance or drafting allowed — a significant logistical step up from a supported gran fondo, requiring real self-sufficiency planning
The previous version of this profile carried mismatched video/rider-intel content from unrelated Dutch road races and an unrelated wind-cancellation clip, none of which described this event
biased_opinion.bottom_line: If you want an active self-supported ultra-distance test through the Netherlands, this is real — just understand it's an unsupported multi-day race, not a one-day fondo, and the next classic-loop edition isn't until 2028.

final_verdict.one_liner: An active self-supported ultra loop through the Netherlands — currently biennial, and not a gran fondo.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes, if you specifically want a self-supported, unsupported multi-day ultra-distance test and understand the format (no outside crew or drafting allowed). No, if you're looking for a supported one-day gran fondo — this isn't that, and the next classic full-loop edition isn't until 2028.
final_verdict.alternatives: For a supported one-day Dutch gran fondo instead, look at organized sportive events in the Netherlands rather than this self-supported ultra format. For a comparable self-supported ultra-distance test, see other Adventure Bike Racing / similar-format European loop races.

race._media_note: Removed 2026-07-24 (editorial wave 9): prior youtube_data (videos + rider_intel) entirely described an unrelated event (165km 17-lap cobbled Dutch national championship road race, a Cape Town Cycle Tour wind-cancellation clip, generic Dutch touring footage) and did not depict this ~1,950km self-supported ultra loop race. Removed per wave scope rather than replaced.

catalog_flags.status_note: TAXONOMY MISMATCH: this is a self-supported, unsupported multi-day ultra-distance race (~1,950km, no outside crew/drafting allowed), organized by Adventure Bike Racing, not a single-day gran fondo. fondo_rating dimensions (field_depth, organization, logistics, value, etc.) are built for one-day supported fondos and don't cleanly capture an unsupported ultra format. Recommend a human decide whether ultra-distance events like this need a distinct discipline/taxonomy in this database. Discipline enum left unchanged (gran_fondo) per wave scope.

eligibility.notes: Confirmed active event, organized by Germany-based Adventure Bike Racing (Michael Wacker); De Proloog is the host venue in Amerongen, not the organizing body (the pre-existing profile did not itself misattribute an organizer, it simply had no organizer field). 2026 classic full-loop edition ran May 2-10 as scheduled: started May 2 08:00 from Amerongen, cutoff May 10 18:00 (202 hours), with Tiemen Van Tuinen recorded as first full-loop solo finisher in provisional/unofficial results. Field size well over 50 entrants, correcting the previous '20-50' figure. Next classic full-loop edition scheduled for 6 May 2028; the event is currently on a roughly biennial cadence (earlier editions, 2018-2022, appear to have run more frequently per organizer results archives, though this was not exhaustively verified). A separate gravel variant, RATN GX, is scheduled for 12 June 2027. No cancellation or rebrand signal found.
